boundary: Search judges only channel-concept relevance and same-profile supply. Downstream Source Fit / Source Policy / Vision QA defects (burned-in captions, watermarks, overlays/CTA/end cards, static framing, missing payoff, compilations) never flow back into the search label. This overlay corrects the search-role expectations without touching the shared base annotation identity: conceptRelevant records whether the candidate belongs to the channel concept itself, and searchEventSummary is a neutral content-only pool summary that carries no rejection or usability verdict.

cases.searchEventSummary: A man and a tiger chase, embrace, and roll together in continuous close contact.
cases.note: continuous warm human-animal contact — inside the dark-joy-boy concept

cases.searchEventSummary: A smiling woman carries a chimpanzee on her shoulders while both wave at the camera.
cases.note: warm human-animal contact — inside the dark-joy-boy concept

cases.searchEventSummary: A night crash and fire are compiled together with a separate daytime interview and burned-in subtitles.

cases.searchEventSummary: Two state troopers help a choking baby until the child begins breathing again.

revisionNote: v3 resolves an internal contradiction introduced in v1: the DWwSVVOjMqO label declared the candidate concept-violating as a "compilation", while the copscopes concept bans compilations of UNRELATED incidents and the role-boundary instruction explicitly routes compilation-format concerns to downstream Source Fit. The night crash and its daytime interview cover one story event, so the candidate is concept-relevant for search; its multi-scene format and burned-in subtitles remain downstream fit rejections. conceptRelevant flips to true; the model-independent basis is the original human disposition record and the concept text, and the v3-search-boundary run confirmed both reasoning efforts read it as concept-relevant under the boundary instruction. All other cases unchanged from v2.
